what_it_does: Renders a build intent's persisted blast_radius as an interactive SVG ripple with concentric relationship rings, tick-able member chips, and an operator-only 'approve reach' action that widens the editable scope; supports three aesthetic variants driven by a shared geometry config.
observations: Uses a single VARIANTS config object to switch SVG geometry and layout between looks A, B, and C without duplicating logic.
The visual node density cap (cfg.densityCap) limits drawn ring nodes, but the legend always lists every member so the approval state machine spans the full set.
Default-narrow semantics: the approve button is disabled until at least one member is ticked, so nothing is silently widened.
Colour is token-only via br-ring-* CSS classes; variants add only a wrapper class and geometry values.
Navigation guard restricts onNavigate to ui:// addresses; code:// members are inert pending a reserved design pass.
Heavy inline comments document design rationale, Tim-reserved decisions, and X-series cross-references.
standouts: Three aesthetic variants in one component through a pure geometry config, with shared behaviour.
Operator-only reach approval wired to api.approveReach with optimistic done-set and fail-loud error display.
Visual +N overflow chip on dense rings while the legend preserves full tick-ability.
conventions: React functional component with useState and useMemo hooks.
TypeScript types declared locally for variant config and exported variant key.
CSS class prefix br-* and token-driven styling; no hardcoded colours in TSX.
api abstraction for server calls; errors are surfaced in the UI.
concerns: Top-level data prop d is typed as any, and payload/blast_radius are accessed without runtime validation.
onNavigate is only fired for ui:// strings; the guard is file-local and may leave code:// members non-navigable indefinitely.
Variant choice is local state and not persisted; the prop variant can be overridden by user selection.
SVG nodes use onClick without keyboard handlers or ARIA roles beyond title, which may limit accessibility.
The approve handler adds all ticked members to the optimistic done set only when the API reports no error, but partial failures are not distinguished.
notes: A design-heavy React component that turns a persisted blast radius into a visual, operator-consented scope expansion, with a previewable aesthetic selector and a strict default-narrow policy.
questions: What is the exact shape of d.payload and blast_radius expected from the caller?
How should code:// member addresses be resolved for navigation once the reserved design pass is completed?
Should the variant preview choice be persisted or controlled externally rather than kept as local state?
What does api.approveReach return, and how are partial approvals handled?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it presents the blast radius visually and provides the explicit reach-approval control described in the header comments.
apparent_themes: Operator consent and explicit scope expansion.
Design-system token discipline and reserved design ownership.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make blast-radius scope expansion feel like an inspected, operator-approved ripple rather than an invisible automatic widening, while leaving the final visual styling as a previewable, designer-owned choice.
novelty: A concentric-ring SVG visualization of code relationships combined with a tick-to-approve scope control and a live aesthetic-variant selector backed by a single geometry config.
contribution: Provides a concrete, interactive React surface that renders persisted blast-radius data as an SVG ripple, exposes an explicit operator approval action, and demonstrates a pattern for multiple aesthetic treatments through one shared render path and a geometry-only config.
summary_for_embedding: Interactive React component that visualizes a build intent's blast radius as an SVG ripple with relationship rings and an operator-only reach-approval control, supporting three aesthetic variants via a shared geometry config.
intention_for_embedding: To let an operator inspect related code and explicitly widen a build's editable scope by approving selected blast-radius members.
suggested_fields: design_owner to record reserved design decisions like Tim's reach-surface ownership.
aesthetic_variant_default to capture which variant should be canonical.
operator_action_kind to tag explicit approval controls.

symbols.does: Exported union type for the three aesthetic variant keys.
symbols.description: ReachVariant = 'A' | 'B' | 'C' is the public selector type used by the component prop and the local variant picker.

symbols.does: Internal type describing per-variant SVG geometry and layout parameters.
symbols.description: VariantCfg defines radii, node sizes, density cap, legend placement, and stroke/opacity values; it intentionally excludes colour, which stays in CSS token classes.

symbols.does: Main exported React component rendering the blast-radius reach surface and approval UI.
symbols.description: It reads d.payload.blast_radius and reach_approved, manages ticked/done/error/busy/variant state, renders the SVG ripple and legend, and calls api.approveReach when the operator approves the reach.

symbols.does: Adds or removes a member address from the pending ticked set.
symbols.description: toggle updates local ticked state; it ignores members already in the approved reach so they cannot be re-ticked.

symbols.does: Calls api.approveReach for all currently ticked members.
symbols.description: approve sends the ticked list to the operator-only endpoint, surfaces any error, and on success moves those members into the optimistic done set and clears the ticked set.

symbols.does: Returns true only for ui:// member addresses.
symbols.description: navigable guards onNavigate so that code:// members do not mis-route through the canvas camera path before the reserved navigation design is implemented.

symbols.does: Extracts a short display name from a long member address.
symbols.description: leaf strips state segments (/@...) and returns the final path segment, truncating to 18 characters for the right-rail chip label.

symbols.does: Computes the SVG radius for a relationship ring band.
symbols.description: ringRadius uses the variant config innerR/outerR/bandOffset to place each ring evenly between the centre and the outer edge.

symbols.does: Computes the member-node radius for a ring, optionally scaling by kind.
symbols.description: nodeRadius returns the active radius for ticked/in-reach nodes, and for variant B scales idle nodes larger on inner rings to emphasize heavier relationships.
